inline_comments.message: Was it not possible to do something like was done for node 19? Running curl scripts this way is not really considered best practice

Sadly the old way to setup the installation repo is not working, I tried a similar approach for node 20 and it get following error:

---
The repository 'https://deb.nodesource.com/node_20.x jammy Release' does not have a Release file., W:Updating from such a repository can't be done securely, and is therefore disabled by default., W:See apt-secure(8) manpage for repository creation and user configuration details., W:Skipping acquire of configured file 'main/source/Sources' as repository 'https://deb.nodesource.com/node_22.x nodistro InRelease' does not seem to provide it (sources.list entry misspelt?), W:https://ppa.launchpadcontent.net/openjdk-r/ppa/ubuntu/dists/jammy/InRelease: Key is stored in legacy trusted.gpg keyring (/etc/apt/trusted.gpg), see the DEPRECATION section in apt-key(8) for details
---

Alternatively I solved the DEPRECATION note above but still got a similar error:

---
==> openstack.docker: 188         - name: Add nodejs repository with signed-by
==> openstack.docker: Failed to update apt cache after 5 retries: E:The repository 'https://deb.nodesource.com/node_20.x jammy Release' does not have a Release file., W:Updating from such a repository can't be done securely, and is therefore disabled by default., W:See apt-secure(8) manpage for repository creation and user configuration details., W:Skipping acquire of configured file 'main/source/Sources' as repository 'https://deb.nodesource.com/node_22.x nodistro InRelease' does not seem to provide it (sources.list entry misspelt?), W:https://ppa.launchpadcontent.net/openjdk-r/ppa/ubuntu/dists/jammy/InRelease: Key is stored in legacy trusted.gpg keyring (/etc/apt/trusted.gpg), see the DEPRECATION section in apt-key(8) for details.
==> openstack.docker: 189           ansible.builtin.apt_repository:
==> openstack.docker: 190             repo: "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.asc] https://deb.nodesource.com/node_20.x {{ ansib...
==> openstack.docker:
==> openstack.docker:                       ^ column 19
---

I'm afraid my first approach is the only option that works so far, I will try to find another way without using curl.

Hi @agrimberg@linuxfoundation.org
I found another way to fix this, please review my latest patchset.
